goal: Rebuild foundational strength, address cycling imbalances, support back health
equipment: Squat Rack, Barbell, Olympic Rings, Dumbbells (up to 50 lbs)
coach_note: This program is designed for a gradual return to strength training after time off. As a former Ironman athlete with a cycling background, you likely have excellent aerobic capacity but may have muscle imbalances typical of endurance cyclists: tight hip flexors, weak glutes, underdeveloped posterior chain, and rounded thoracic spine. We're prioritizing core stability, hip mobility, and posterior chain strength to support your back. Start light — the goal for weeks 1-4 is movement quality and building the habit. The ring work will build tremendous core stability and shoulder health.

program_notes.phase_1_weeks_1_4: Foundation Phase — Focus on movement quality, mobility, and building consistency. Keep weights light (RPE 5-6). Master form before adding load.
program_notes.phase_2_weeks_5_8: Build Phase — Gradually increase weights (RPE 6-7). Add sets where indicated. Progress ring movements to harder variations.
program_notes.back_care: Start barbell movements with just the bar or light weight. All core work emphasizes stability (anti-movement). If any exercise aggravates your back, stop and notify your coach.
program_notes.ring_progressions: Rings are unstable — start with easier angles. For rows: more upright = easier. For push-ups: rings higher = easier. Progress by lowering angle over time.
program_notes.recovery: Sleep 7-8 hours. Foam roll and stretch daily, especially hip flexors and thoracic spine.

schedule.warmup: 5 min jump rope or jog in place + Cat-Cow (10), Hip Circles (10 each), Glute Bridges (15), Bird Dogs (10 each), 90/90 Hip Stretch (60s each)

exercises.name: Glute Bridge (floor)
exercises.notes: Squeeze glutes 2 sec at top, don't hyperextend back

exercises.name: Goblet Squat (DB)
exercises.notes: Hold DB at chest, sit between legs, chest tall

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (BB)
exercises.notes: Start with bar only, hinge at hips, soft knees, feel hamstrings

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge (DB)
exercises.notes: Step back, front knee tracks over toe, drive through front heel

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.notes: Keep pelvis level, drive through heel

exercises.name: DB Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Hold DBs, full ROM, pause at top

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
exercises.notes: Hip external rotation — keep pelvis stable

schedule.cooldown: Hip flexor stretch (90s each), Pigeon pose (60s each), Lying spinal twist (60s each)

schedule.warmup: 5 min light movement + Arm Circles (20 each), Band Pull-Aparts or Ring Rows x5 (easy), Wall Angels (10), Thoracic Rotations (8 each)

exercises.name: Ring Push-Up (elevated)
exercises.notes: Rings at hip height to start, lower over weeks. Core tight, full ROM

exercises.name: Ring Row
exercises.notes: Body more upright = easier. Squeeze shoulder blades, opens chest

exercises.name: DB Overhead Press (seated on bench/floor)
exercises.notes: Neutral grip option if shoulders feel better, brace core

exercises.name: DB Incline Press (floor or bench)
exercises.notes: Prop on pillow/pad for slight incline if no bench

exercises.name: Ring Face Pull
exercises.notes: Lean back, pull rings to face, external rotate — posture corrector

exercises.name: DB Tricep Overhead Extension
exercises.notes: Single DB, both hands, keep elbows in

exercises.name: Ring Support Hold
exercises.notes: Lock arms, turn rings out slightly, build shoulder stability

schedule.cooldown: Doorway pec stretch (30s each), Child's pose (60s), Hang from bar or rings (30-60s)

schedule.warmup: 5-10 min easy walk or bike + Full body stretch flow (5 min)

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Press low back into floor, move slowly — core stability king

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.notes: Anti-rotation, extend opposite arm/leg, keep hips level

exercises.name: Ring Fallout (kneeling)
exercises.notes: Like ab wheel — extend arms, maintain hollow body, don't collapse

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.notes: From knees if needed, stack hips, don't let hips sag

exercises.name: Glute Bridge March
exercises.notes: Hold bridge, lift one foot at a time — pelvic stability

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.notes: Slow and controlled, feel each vertebra move

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.notes: Hip flexor + thoracic rotation — essential for cyclists

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Transitions
exercises.notes: Improve hip internal/external rotation

exercises.name: Ring Hang (passive)
exercises.notes: Decompress spine, relax shoulders, breathe deep

schedule.cooldown: Supine spinal twist (60s each), Happy baby pose (60s), Deep breathing (2 min)

schedule.warmup: 5 min light cardio + Leg Swings (10 each), Bodyweight Squats (10), Single-Leg Balance (30s each), Ankle Circles (10 each)

exercises.name: Goblet Squat (DB)
exercises.notes: Sit between legs, chest up, controlled tempo

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Light weight, hinge pattern, hamstring focus

exercises.name: DB Step-Up (low surface)
exercises.notes: Use bench/box, drive through front heel only

exercises.name: DB Walking Lunge
exercises.notes: Control each step, upright torso

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL (DB)
exercises.notes: Balance and hamstring strength — hold rack if needed

exercises.name: DB Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Wide stance, toes out, inner thigh and glute focus

exercises.name: Calf Raise (barbell on back)
exercises.notes: Light bar, full ROM, slow eccentric

schedule.cooldown: Standing quad stretch (30s each), Forward fold (60s), Figure-4 stretch (60s each)

schedule.warmup: 5 min light movement + Scapular Rows on Rings (10), Arm Circles (20 each), Prone Y-T-W (8 each), Shoulder CARS (5 each)

exercises.name: Ring Row (feet elevated)
exercises.notes: Harder angle — pull chest to rings, squeeze at top

exercises.name: Barbell Row (bent over)
exercises.notes: Light weight, chest supported angle if back sensitive, pull to belly

exercises.name: Ring Face Pull
exercises.notes: High pull, external rotate — can't do too many of these

exercises.name: DB Reverse Fly (bent over)
exercises.notes: Rear delts and mid-traps — posture muscles

exercises.name: DB Curl
exercises.notes: Controlled, no swinging, supinate at top

exercises.name: Ring Inverted Hang (tuck)
exercises.notes: Hang inverted, knees tucked, decompress spine

exercises.name: Ring Support Hold
exercises.notes: Arms locked, rings turned out, build stability

exercises.name: Passive Hang (bar or rings)
exercises.notes: Spinal decompression — relax and breathe

schedule.cooldown: Lat stretch on rack (30s each), Cross-body shoulder stretch (30s each), Thoracic extension over foam roller (2 min)
